21.correct: The First Constitutional Amendment Act was passed in 1951, adding the Ninth Schedule and reasonable restrictions on free speech.

wrong.B: 1952 was the year of the first general elections, not the first amendment.

21.fact: The First Amendment (1951) created the Ninth Schedule to protect land-reform laws from judicial review.

22.correct: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha can be removed by a resolution passed by a majority of all the then members of the Lok Sabha.

wrong.A: The President does not remove the Speaker.
wrong.B: The Prime Minister cannot remove the Speaker.
wrong.C: A joint sitting is not used to remove the Speaker.

22.fact: A 14-day notice is required before moving such a resolution.

23.correct: Kuchipudi, a classical dance form, originated in Andhra Pradesh.

wrong.A: Tamil Nadu is home to Bharatanatyam.

23.fact: Kuchipudi is named after a village in Andhra Pradesh and combines dance and drama.

24.correct: A lactometer works on Archimedes' principle - it measures the density (and hence purity) of milk by how it floats.
24.fact: A lactometer is a type of hydrometer calibrated for milk.

25.correct: Mulk Raj Anand's novel 'Across the Black Waters' deals with Indian soldiers fighting in the First World War.

wrong.A: It is not about the Partition of India.
wrong.C: It is not about tribal life.
wrong.D: It is not about post-independence urban poverty.

25.fact: It is part of Mulk Raj Anand's trilogy following the character Lalu.

26.correct: India won the first-ever ICC T20 World Cup in 2007 (in South Africa), under captain M.S. Dhoni.

wrong.D: 2011 was when India won the ODI World Cup.

26.fact: India beat Pakistan in the final of the inaugural 2007 T20 World Cup.

27.correct: Squamous (flat) epithelium lines the inner surface of the oesophagus and mouth, protecting against friction/abrasion.

wrong.A: Columnar epithelium lines the stomach and intestine.
wrong.C: Cuboidal epithelium lines glands and tubules.

27.fact: Squamous epithelium is thin and flat, suited to areas of wear and tear.

28.correct: A key step in India's post-1991 trade liberalization was abolishing the import-licensing system for most goods (ending the 'Licence Raj' in trade).

wrong.A: Imposing high tariffs is protectionist, not liberalizing.
wrong.C: Increasing quantitative restrictions is the opposite of liberalization.
wrong.D: Fixing export quotas is not liberalization.

28.fact: The 1991 reforms removed licensing and reduced tariffs to open up the economy.

29.correct: In American football, a 'field goal' is worth 3 points.

wrong.A: 1 point is an extra-point conversion after a touchdown.
wrong.B: 2 points is a two-point conversion or a safety.

29.fact: A field goal is scored by kicking the ball through the opponents' goalposts.

30.correct: India's first Economic Survey was presented for 1950-51.
30.fact: From 1964, the Economic Survey began to be presented separately, just before the Union Budget.

31.correct: India Post acts as a catalyst for the rural economy through digital services and microcredit/financial-inclusion expansion (e.g. the India Post Payments Bank).

wrong.B: It does not provide water supply.
wrong.C: It is not primarily a health-insurance provider.
wrong.D: Telecom services are not its main rural-economy role here.

31.fact: The India Post Payments Bank extends banking to remote rural areas.

32.correct: Solar energy is classified as a non-conventional (renewable) energy resource in India.

wrong.B: Natural gas is a conventional fuel.

32.fact: Non-conventional sources include solar, wind, tidal, geothermal and biomass energy.

33.correct: By June 2025, the number of Jan Dhan accounts had grown to about 55.22 crore.
33.fact: The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (2014) drove financial inclusion through zero-balance accounts.

34.correct: Kanishka, the Kushan emperor, is famous in history as a great patron of Buddhism.

wrong.A: He is not chiefly known as a patron of Jainism.
wrong.D: He is not associated with Zoroastrianism patronage.

34.fact: Kanishka convened the Fourth Buddhist Council and promoted Mahayana Buddhism.

35.correct: As of May 2025, India had over 1,013 km of operational metro lines, improving urban connectivity.
35.fact: India has one of the fastest-growing metro-rail networks in the world.

36.correct: The Apollo programme (including the Apollo missions) belongs to the USA (NASA).

wrong.A: It is not a French programme.
wrong.B: It is not a Chinese programme.
wrong.C: It is not an Indian programme.

36.fact: NASA's Apollo 11 achieved the first crewed Moon landing in 1969.

37.correct: The British passed the Hindu Widows' Remarriage Act in 1856, legalising the remarriage of Hindu widows.

wrong.D: 1885 is the year the Congress was founded.

37.fact: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar campaigned strongly for widow remarriage.

38.correct: In the last (low stationary) stage of the demographic transition, both fertility (I) and mortality (II) are low, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are low.

38.fact: The low-stationary stage is typical of developed countries with stable populations.

39.correct: In tennis, if both players win 6 games each in a set, the set goes into a tie-breaker.

wrong.A: 4 games each does not trigger a tie-breaker.
wrong.D: At 7 games one player has usually already won (or it went to a tie-break at 6-6).

39.fact: A standard tie-break is first to 7 points (with a 2-point margin).

40.correct: Kerala secured the first rank in the Urban Governance Index (UGI) 2024.

wrong.A: Haryana did not top the index.
wrong.C: Tamil Nadu did not top it.
wrong.D: Himachal Pradesh did not top it.

40.fact: Kerala consistently performs well on governance and human-development indices.
